response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r0c3 is in critical survival mode: islanded with only 9% state of charge (0.27/3 kWh) and a 1.0 kW load that will exhaust reserves in ~16 minutes. The memory log shows a sustained cascade of incoming requests from r0c2 (urgent), r0c4, and r1c3 (normal priority), all reporting SoC fractions between 22–26%, while r0c2 has explicitly rejected outbound sharing citing cluster-wide cascading failure risk. The observation that SoC has remained flat at 0.27 kWh across multiple timestamps despite active load suggests either measurement lag or that the agent is already in a controlled shutdown state. The policy parse failures and reliance on fallback round-robin logic indicate system instability. Given that r0c3 itself is below any reasonable sharing threshold and peers are also critically depleted, the only viable strategy is to (1) cease all outbound sharing immediately to preserve the minimal survival margin, (2) signal urgent inbound need to any peers still capable of supply, and (3) accept that cluster-wide energy starvation may be unavoidable without external grid reconnection. Trust circles are empty, so no preferential allocation is possible; geographic proximity (r0c2, r0c4 as row-0 neighbors) should be weighted slightly higher than r1c3, but all requests should be treated as equally desperate.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "r0c3 critically islanded at 9% SoC with 1.0 kW load; cluster-wide cascading failure active (r0c2 rejected sharing, all peers <26% SoC). Outbound sharing suspended to preserve survival margin. Inbound requests from r0c2 (urgent), r0c4, r1c3 (normal) cannot be fulfilled; awaiting grid reconnection or external supply. Policy parse failures suggest fallback unreliability—manual intervention recommended."
ttl_ticks: 1
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
